description: Machine-readable command catalog for the MiniGraph Playground DSL. Mirrors command-reference.md. An AI agent should ingest this to generate and validate commands deterministically before dispatching them via the companion endpoint (POST /api/graph/{id} for execution; POST /api/companion/{session-id} for live Playground commands).

lexical.node_type: a descriptive label; examples capitalize structural types (Root, End, Provider, Dictionary, Fetcher, Island)

reserved_names.root: the root/entry node must be named 'root'
reserved_names.end: the end/exit node must be named 'end'

lexical.property: key=value; keys may be composite using dot-bracket notation
lexical.list_property: a 'key[]=entry' line appends one entry to the list 'key'; repeat to add more
lexical.multiline_value: wrap a value in triple single quotes ''' ... ''' to span lines
lexical.constant: Event Script constant syntax type(value), e.g. text(hello), int(100); full set in the Event Script docs
lexical.mapping_operator: source -> target (left is source, right is target)

namespaces.name: response.*
namespaces.meaning: a data Provider's raw HTTP response body

commands.name: create node
notes: zero or one skill via skill={route}
commands.example:
create node end
with type End
with properties
skill=graph.data.mapper
mapping[]=text(hello world) -> output.body

commands.name: update node
notes: same shape as create node; replaces the node definition
commands.example:
update node end
with type End
with properties
skill=graph.data.mapper
mapping[]=input.body -> output.body

commands.name: connect
commands.example: connect root to end with done

commands.name: instantiate graph
commands.example:
instantiate graph
int(100) -> input.body.profile_id
text(application/json) -> input.header.content-type

commands.name: execute
notes: run a single node after instantiate

commands.name: export graph
notes: fails if any node is an orphan

commands.name: session
commands.syntax: session | session subscribe {id} | session unsubscribe | session reset

node_types.role: label validated by the node's skill, if any

notes: full JavaScript via GraalVM; max 50 instances; prefer graph.math for simple math

skills.example:
skill=graph.api.fetcher
dictionary[]=person-name
input[]=input.body.person_id -> person_id
output[]=result.name -> output.body.name

notes: extension={graph-id} for a sub-graph, or flow://{flow-id} for an Event Script flow
skills.example:
skill=graph.extension
extension=evaluate-sales-performance
input[]=input.body.department_id -> id
output[]=result -> output.body

notes: returns next only when all upstream nodes complete, else .sink (pause)

invariants: root node named 'root'; end node named 'end'
node names are lowercase + hyphen only (types are descriptive labels, commonly capitalized)
every node in the traversal path must connect to >=1 node or export fails
Dictionary/Provider config nodes are referenced by name (not traversed) and need no connections; optionally grouped under a graph.island
a node is executed once per run (RESET in graph.math/graph.js is the only escape)
